title: Spergularia pentandra
taxonomy: Spergularia pentandra is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Spergularia, which is a group of annual or perennial plants commonly known as sand spurreys. The Caryophyllaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as the pink or carnation family. This family includes a wide range of species, from small annuals to large perennials. Spergularia pentandra is native to Europe and Western Asia.

intro: Spergularia pentandra, also known as sea sand-spurrey, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Spergularia pentandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
